# 'branch create' when trunk is checked out in another worktree.
# Creating a new branch based on trunk should work
# even if trunk is checked out elsewhere.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# create feature1 off of main
git add feature1.txt
gs bc feature1 -m 'Add feature1'

# create a worktree for main
git worktree add ../wt-main main

# From feature1 in the main repo,
# create a new branch based on trunk.
# This should succeed even though main
# is checked out in another worktree.
git add feature2.txt
gs bc feature2 --target main -m 'Add feature2'

# Verify the branch was created with the correct base.
gs ls -a
stderr 'feature2'

# Verify the branch graph.
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
feature1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
feature2
-- golden/graph.txt --
* d1dd13f (feature1) Add feature1
| * 1d740bb (HEAD -> feature2) Add feature2
|/  
* e2cefa6 (main) Initial commit
